message:
fix(vdbe): fresh-eyes review — 5 bug fixes from comprehensive code audit

Fixes found by systematic fresh-eyes review of all 26 session commits:

1. CRITICAL: FusedAppendInsert missing mark_storage_table_dirty()
   The fused insert handler didn't mark the table as dirty after writing,
   causing subsequent MemDB fast-path queries (COUNT(*), LIKE) to read
   stale data. Regular Insert calls sync_storage_table_insert_into_memdb_mirror
   which calls mark_storage_table_dirty — the fused path skipped this.

2. CRITICAL: CursorSlots negative ID wrapping to usize::MAX
   If a negative cursor ID was passed, `id as usize` wraps to usize::MAX,
   causing ensure_slot to attempt a Vec resize to usize::MAX+1 (OOM panic)
   or get/get_mut to silently return None. Added id < 0 guards to all
   CursorSlots methods (get, get_mut, insert, remove, contains_key).

3. HIGH: TransactionKind::Drained panic message improved
   The panic message now explains exactly what happened (a retained cursor
   tried to access pages while the txn was extracted) and when it occurs
   (between drain_transaction and refill_transaction). The panic is correct
   behavior (invariant violation) but the message was too terse.

4. HIGH: ProgramBuilder::finish() now asserts last opcode is Halt
   The V2.3 bounds check elimination in the VDBE dispatch loop relies on
   every program ending with Halt. Previously this was a convention with
   only a debug_assert in the loop. Now ProgramBuilder::finish() validates
   the invariant at compile time with a hard assert.

5. MEDIUM: FusedAppendInsert returns error instead of silent skip
   When the storage cursor is missing or not writable, the handler now
   returns a clear error instead of silently doing nothing (which would
   cause data loss — the INSERT would be silently dropped).

All 20 V1+V2 regression tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
